stress.where: The Linear Representation Hypothesis / 'one concept = one direction' (Park et al. LRH; Arditi et al. 2024 'Refusal is mediated by a single direction'; Engels et al. 2024 'Not All Language Model Features Are Linear'; Wollschläger et al. 2025 'The Geometry of Refusal'; Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry)
stress.why: Single-direction claims keep getting revised into cones, circles, manifolds, and multi-dimensional subspaces: refusal turned out to be a cone of directions, days-of-week are circular, belief states are fractal. Each counterexample is patched locally ('multi-dimensional features') without revising the core concept. 'Direction' is itself overloaded: the probe direction, the best steering direction, and the SAE decoder row for the 'same' concept demonstrably differ, and the field has no principled way to say which one IS the representation.

concept.name: Interface Pairing (reader–writer dual representation)
concept.definition: A concept's representation at layer L is not one vector but an ordered pair of subspaces plus their coupling: the WRITER subspace W_c = top-k right singular vectors of the activation-difference matrix over contrast pairs (x+, x-), and the READER subspace R_c = top-k singular vectors of the downstream Jacobian (gradient of the behavior metric, e.g. refusal logit-diff, w.r.t. activations at L) over the same pairs. Report the representation as (W_c, R_c, principal angles between them). Probe direction, steering direction, and SAE decoder row are then classified as reader-side or writer-side objects — the overloading of 'direction' dissolves by construction.
concept.prediction: Computable on Llama-3-8B refusal: the causal effect (change in refusal logit-diff) of an arbitrary unit-norm perturbation v at layer L is predicted by ||proj_{R_c}(v)|| with r^2 >= 0.8 across random v, while ||proj_{W_c}(v)|| predicts with r^2 lower by >= 0.2 whenever the first principal angle between R_c and W_c exceeds ~20 degrees. Corollary: Arditi et al.'s best steering vector lies closer (cosine) to R_c than to the difference-of-means direction. FALSIFIED if writer-side projection predicts intervention effects as well as reader-side projection across concepts and models (i.e., the pairing adds nothing over one vector).
concept.reduces_to: The 'reading vs writing weights' folklore in Transformer Circuits (virtual weights, QK vs OV), plus gradient-based attribution — risk that it is just 'use the gradient as the steering direction' with extra formalism.

test.design: Rescoped honestly from Llama-3-8B refusal (out of CPU budget) to gpt2-small, L8 resid_pre, last token, 6 word-category concepts with a category-contrast logit metric — same logical structure (writer=deposit, reader=consumption, causal probe=perturbation). R_c = top-4 SVD of behavior gradients over 6 base prompts (linearized 'what downstream reads'); W_c = top-4 SVD of concept source-prompt activations minus grand mean ('what upstream deposits'). ONE intervention: x' = x + alpha_c*v (alpha_c = natural concept-writing scale), n=80 stratified random unit v per concept (Dirichlet mass over R/W/ambient components so projections vary). Per concept: r2 of |E(v)| ~ ||proj_R(v)|| vs ~ ||proj_W(v)||; first principal angle theta1 gates eligibility (>20 deg). PREREGISTERED and committed (maevtica c440d26) BEFORE running: SUPPORTED iff median r2R >= 0.8 AND median (r2R - r2W) over eligible >= 0.2; REFUTED iff median r2R < 0.5 OR median D <= 0.05 (writer parity = pairing adds nothing); else INCONCLUSIVE. Arditi-vector corollary: designed_not_run (needs Llama-3-8B + refusal set; design noted in PREREG). Files: /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/interface_pairing/{PREREG.md,exp.py,RESULT.txt}, results commit 54ff5a8.
test.numbers: Median r2R = 0.246 (per-concept 0.198-0.323) vs claimed >= 0.8 → REFUTED by the frozen medR<0.5 clause. Median r2W = 0.019; median D = 0.229 with all 6/6 groups eligible (theta1 = 65-73 deg) — so the prediction's own kill clause (writer predicts as well) did NOT fire: reader decisively beats writer, but the unsigned projection NORM onto the 4-d reader subspace is nowhere near an r2=0.8 predictor because it discards orientation within the subspace. The signed 1-d predictor |g_mean·v| hits r2 = 0.77-0.93 (median 0.91). E(diff-in-means steering dir) = +2.6 to +6.9 logits (steering itself works). Honest scope: one small model, one layer, additive last-token perturbation only — 'refuted in this regime', and the directional half (effects live reader-side) actually survived.
test.sanity: All oracles green before any verdict was read: O0 batched start_at_layer forward matches the full forward to 0.00000 logits; O1 positive control effect = 8.48 logits mean |E(top reader dir)| (measurement produces large real effects, alpha not too small); O2 projection code: in-subspace vectors project to 1.0000, ambient random to 0.0754 (theory: sqrt(4/768)=0.072) — exactly the check that guards the .norm-bug class; O3: E(+r1)/E(-r1) sign-flip 6/6, and the r2lin = 0.91 ceiling proves the regression pipeline CAN yield high r2 with the right predictor — so the low r2R is a fact about the concept, not a broken run.

concept.name: Concept-as-Operator (orbit representation)
concept.definition: A concept family (days-of-week, months, count increments) is represented by the minimal-rank linear operator T acting on the residual stream, not by any vector: fit T minimizing rank r subject to ||h(x incremented by c) - T h(x)|| / ||h(x incremented by c) - h(x)|| <= epsilon on held-out paired contexts. The feature's observed 'shape' is the orbit structure of T's eigen-spectrum: pure translation gives a line (classic LRH), a complex eigenpair on the unit circle gives a circle, rotation+scaling gives a cone/spiral. Cones and circles stop being patches — they are the operator taxonomy.
concept.prediction: Computable on GPT-2/Gemma day-of-week tokens: a single rank-2 orthogonal operator fit ONLY on Monday-to-Tuesday context pairs predicts Wednesday-to-Thursday activation deltas with cosine >= 0.8 (orbit generalization), and its rotation eigenvalue argument is within 10% of 2*pi/7; the best fixed 'add one direction' model fit on the same pairs generalizes with materially lower cosine. FALSIFIED if the vector-addition model matches the operator model on held-out orbit steps, or if the fitted eigenvalue argument is unrelated to 2*pi/7.
concept.reduces_to: Function vectors / task vectors (Todd et al., Hendel et al. 2023-24) and the Fourier-circuit analysis of modular arithmetic (Nanda et al.) — risk it is 'function vectors, but for features'.

test.design: Model: gpt2 (CPU, transformer_lens), resid_post at the day token (last position) for 32 sentence templates x {Monday..Thursday}; 24 train / 8 held-out templates (seed 0). Operator: rank-2 rotation fit ONLY on train Mon->Tue pairs — plane = top-2 SVD of the delta matrix, in-plane 2x2 rotation via Procrustes (det=+1), identity off-plane. Baseline: best fixed add-one-direction vector = mean(Tue-Mon) on the same pairs. Layer chosen 1..11 by held-out Mon->Tue oracle only (no Wed/Thu leakage). Preregistered rule (frozen before running, /tmp/.../scratchpad/PREREG_concept_operator_orbit.md): oracle gate = held-out Mon->Tue operator cosine >= 0.5 else BROKEN_MEASUREMENT; SUPPORTED iff Wed->Thu operator cosine >= 0.8 AND | |theta| - 2pi/7 | / (2pi/7) <= 0.10 AND operator beats vector by >= 0.10; REFUTED iff vector matches operator (gap < 0.05) OR angle rel-error > 0.25; else INCONCLUSIVE. Robustness variant also run: same fit with activations centered at the train Mon/Tue grand mean (rotation about circle-ish center instead of origin). Scripts: orbit_operator_test.py, orbit_centered_variant.py in the scratchpad.
test.numbers: Selected layer 3 (best oracle): held-out Wed->Thu operator cosine = 0.278 (needed >= 0.8; max across all layers 0.35 origin-fit / 0.39 centered-fit); vector baseline = -0.342 (gap +0.62, so the operator DID materially beat vector addition); fitted |theta| = 3.096 rad vs target 2pi/7 = 0.898 — rel error 2.45 (245%, needed <= 10%). Centered variant: theta = 3.1406 ~ pi at every layer 1-9, Wed->Thu cosine 0.29-0.39, angle rel error ~2.5 everywhere. The fitted "rotation" degenerates to a pi-flip absorbing the mean translation; consecutive-day deltas even anticorrelate (cos ~ -0.34) where a heptagon orbit predicts +0.62.
test.sanity: Oracle PASSED: the fitted operator reproduces held-out Mon->Tue deltas (the transition it was trained on, unseen templates) with mean cosine 0.976 (origin fit, layer 3) and 0.989 (centered fit) — the pipeline, activation extraction, plane fit, and cosine metric all demonstrably work; the Wed->Thu failure is a real property of the representation, not a broken measurement.

backlogLeft: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)
